rulings.Case Title: RODOLFO SOLIS VS. WALTER VARELA JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT As To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-10-2026, Line 1: DEFENDANTS WALTER VARELA, and COMAPAN, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT As To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint. Off calendar. Notice of settlement of entire case filed on March 9, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: PERRY ZHANG ET AL VS. SPROUTS FARMERS MARKET, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Seal The Exhibits In Plaintiffs' Opposition To Defendantsf Markets, Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-10-2026, Line 2: DEFENDANT SF MARKETS, LLC'S, DBA SPROUTS FARMERS MARKET (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS "SPROUTS FARMERS MERKET, INC.") Motion To Seal The Exhibits In Plaintiffs' Opposition To Defendantsf Markets, Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment. The unopposed motion to seal is granted. The court finds that the records sought to be sealed contain private information concerning third parties. The court finds that (1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: KISHA ESCUDERO ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike, Or In The Alternative, Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-10-2026, Line 4: DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'S, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, Motion To Strike, Or In The Alternative, Tax Costs. The motion to strike or tax costs is denied. "If the items appearing in a cost bill appear to be proper charges, the burden is on the party seeking to tax costs to show that they were not reasonable or necessary. On the other hand, if the items are properly objected to, they are put in issue and the burden of proof is on the party claiming them as costs." (Ladas v. California State Auto. Assn. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774; Litt v. Eisenhower Medical Center (2015) 237 Cal. App. 4th 1217, 1224 [when the cost item appears proper on its face, the burden is on the objecting party to show the costs to be unnecessary or unreasonable].) Here, Code of Civil Procedure, section 1033.5, subdivision (a)(1), contemplates recovery of deposition costs. Defendant offers no declaration that depositions did not occur in this case. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: KARENA APPLE FENG VS. FIFTH THIRD BANK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-10-2026, Line 9: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. Plaintiff Karena Apple Feng's application for a preliminary injunction ordering the return of the 2014 Mercedes-Benz ML350 4MATIC (VIN: 4JGDA5HBOEA374039) that is the subject of this suit ("Car") is denied. The court dissolves the temporary restraining order previously issued on January 22, 2026. In deciding whether to issue interim relief, the court must evaluate two interrelated factors. "The first is the likelihood that the plaintiff will prevail on the merits at trial. The second is the interim harm that the plaintiff is likely to sustain if the restraining order were denied as compared to the harm that the defendant is likely to suffer if the order were issued. [] The trial court's determination must be guided by a mix of the potential-merit and interim-harm factors; the greater the plaintiff's showing on one, the less must be shown on the other to support a restraining order. Of course, the scope of available preliminary relief is necessarily limited by the scope of the relief likely to be obtained at trial on the merits. A trial court may not grant a restraining order, regardless of the balance of interim harm, unless there is some possibility that the plaintiff would ultimately prevail on the merits of the claim." (Church of Christ in Hollywood v. Superior Court (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 1244, 1251-1252 [citation simplified].) Defendant Fifth Third Bank N.A. provides evidence that Feng is in default on her loan agreement on the car. (DeJong Decl., para. 4 & Ex. B.) Feng offers no contrary evidence. The court concludes that Feng has not shown a possibility of prevailing on the merits at trial of her claim that the car was improperly seized. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK VS. OLIVER MAULE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-10-2026, Line 10: DEFENDANT OLIVER MAULE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. (PART ONE OF TWO) Defendant Oliver Maule's motion to quash is denied. When a defendant moves to quash service of process, the plaintiff bears the burden of demonstrating facts justifying the exercise of jurisdiction including proper service. (Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1439-1440 ["[T]he burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service"].) Since 1969, statutes governing service are "liberally construed to effectuate service and uphold jurisdiction if actual notice has been received by the defendant." (Pasadena Medi-Center Associates v. Superior Court (1973) 9 Cal.3d 773, 778 [citation simplified].) "The Supreme Court's admonition to construe the process statutes liberally extends to substituted service as well as to personal service." (Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387, 1392.) Here, the court concludes that plaintiff effectuated personal service. Mr. Maule does not deny that he was on the premises on January 24, 2026, at the time of the attempted service. He also declares that no co-resident resides at the premises. Process server Smith declares that he triggered the ringcam, saw an individual inside respond through a window, and the individual attempted to ignore him and refused the documents. The process server called to the defendant by name and then left the papers at defendant's door. Based on this record, the only reasonable inference is that Mr. Maule was at the premises and attempted to avoid service. ***END OF PART ONE OF TWO*** =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK VS. OLIVER MAULE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT OILVER MAULE'S MOTION TO QUASH (ADDED FOR THE POSTING OF THE COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, Apr-10-2026, Line 10: DEFENDANT OLIVER MAULE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. (PART TWO OF TWO) Actual physical delivery of process is not necessary when a defendant is being recalcitrant. Cases recognize the sufficiency of attempted delivery in the presence of a defendant who refuses to accept the papers. (See In re Ball (1934) 2 Cal.App.2d 578, 579 [process server came within twelve fee of petitioner, said "I have here another one of those things for you," tossed paper toward the petitioner, and added "Now you are served"]; Trujillo v. Trujillo (1945) 71 Cal.App.2d 257, 260 [process server loudly explained nature of document through locked door of defendant's car and placed it under windshield wiper].) This case presents similar circumstances to these pre-1969 cases. Since service statutes are now liberally construed, the court finds proper personal service and denies the motion. Defendant shall respond to the complaint in compliance with CCP 418.10(b). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
